classes.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.webapp.WorkManagerWebApp.WorkManagerWebApp

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.webapp.WorkManagerWebApp.WorkManagerWebApp.__init__
documentation:
Create an instance of `WorkManagerWebApp` to manage a release module web request.

> ```
> param 
>
> dictionary storing parameter information from the web request.
> Storage model for GET and POST parameter data is a dictionary of lists.
>
> param 
>
> boolean flag to activate verbose logging.
>
> param 
>
> stream for logging.
> ```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.webapp.WorkManagerWebApp.WorkManagerWebApp.doOp
documentation:
Execute request and package results in response dictionary.

:Returns:
     A dictionary containing response data for the input request.
     Minimally, the content of this dictionary will include the
     keys: CONTENT_TYPE and REQUEST_STRING.

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.webapp.WorkManagerWebApp.WorkManagerWebApp.__dumpRequest
documentation:
Utility method to format the contents of the internal parameter dictionary
containing data from the input web request.

```
Returns

list of formatted text lines
```
Handle request and response object processing for release module web application.

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.webapp.WorkManagerWebApp.WorkManagerWebAppWorker.__init__
documentation:
Worker methods for the chemical component editor application

Performs URL - application mapping and application launching
for chemical component editor tool.

All operations can be driven from this interface which can
supplied with control information from web application request
or from a testing application.

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.webapp.WorkManagerWebApp.WorkManagerWebAppWorker.doOp
documentation:
Map operation to path and invoke operation.

:Returns:

Operation output is packaged in a ResponseContent() object.

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.webapp.WorkManagerWebApp.WorkManagerWebAppWorker.__doOpException
documentation:
Map operation to path and invoke operation.  Exceptions are caught within this method.

:Returns:

Operation output is packaged in a ResponseContent() object.
"""Map operation to path and invoke operation.  No exception handling is performed.
Operation output is packaged in a ResponseContent() object.
# bail out if operation is unknown -

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.webapp.WorkManagerWebApp.WorkManagerWebAppWorker.__processTemplate
documentation:
Read the input HTML template data file and perform the key/value substitutions in the
input parameter dictionary.

```
Params

parameterDict: dictionary where
key = name of subsitution placeholder in the template and
value = data to be used to substitute information for the placeholder

Returns

string representing entirety of content with subsitution placeholders now replaced with data
```

classes.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.__init__
documentation:
Create a new ordered dictionary. Cannot init from a normal dict,
nor from kwargs, since items order is undefined in those cases.

If the `strict` keyword argument is `True` (`False` is the
default) then when doing slice assignment - the `OrderedDict` you are
assigning from *must not* contain any keys in the remaining dict.

```pycon
>>> OrderedDict()
OrderedDict([])
>>> OrderedDict({1: 1})
Traceback (most recent call last):
TypeError: undefined order, cannot get items from dict
>>> OrderedDict({1: 1}.items())
OrderedDict([(1, 1)])
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d
OrderedDict([(1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)])
>>> OrderedDict(d)
OrderedDict([(1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)])
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.__delitem__
documentation:
```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> del d[3]
>>> d
OrderedDict([(1, 3), (2, 1)])
>>> del d[3]
Traceback (most recent call last):
KeyError: 3
>>> d[3] = 2
>>> d
OrderedDict([(1, 3), (2, 1), (3, 2)])
>>> del d[0:1]
>>> d
OrderedDict([(2, 1), (3, 2)])
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.__eq__
documentation:
```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d == OrderedDict(d)
True
>>> d == OrderedDict(((1, 3), (2, 1), (3, 2)))
False
>>> d == OrderedDict(((1, 0), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
False
>>> d == OrderedDict(((0, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
False
>>> d == dict(d)
False
>>> d == False
False
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.__lt__
documentation:
```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> c = OrderedDict(((0, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> c < d
True
>>> d < c
False
>>> d < dict(c)
Traceback (most recent call last):
TypeError: Can only compare with other OrderedDicts
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.__le__
documentation:
```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> c = OrderedDict(((0, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> e = OrderedDict(d)
>>> c <= d
True
>>> d <= c
False
>>> d <= dict(c)
Traceback (most recent call last):
TypeError: Can only compare with other OrderedDicts
>>> d <= e
True
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.__ne__
documentation:
```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d != OrderedDict(d)
False
>>> d != OrderedDict(((1, 3), (2, 1), (3, 2)))
True
>>> d != OrderedDict(((1, 0), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
True
>>> d == OrderedDict(((0, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
False
>>> d != dict(d)
True
>>> d != False
True
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.__gt__
documentation:
```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> c = OrderedDict(((0, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d > c
True
>>> c > d
False
>>> d > dict(c)
Traceback (most recent call last):
TypeError: Can only compare with other OrderedDicts
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.__ge__
documentation:
```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> c = OrderedDict(((0, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> e = OrderedDict(d)
>>> c >= d
False
>>> d >= c
True
>>> d >= dict(c)
Traceback (most recent call last):
TypeError: Can only compare with other OrderedDicts
>>> e >= d
True
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.__repr__
documentation:
Used for \_\_repr\_\_ and \_\_str\_\_

```pycon
>>> r1 = repr(OrderedDict((('a', 'b'), ('c', 'd'), ('e', 'f'))))
>>> r1
"OrderedDict([('a', 'b'), ('c', 'd'), ('e', 'f')])"
>>> r2 = repr(OrderedDict((('a', 'b'), ('e', 'f'), ('c', 'd'))))
>>> r2
"OrderedDict([('a', 'b'), ('e', 'f'), ('c', 'd')])"
>>> r1 == str(OrderedDict((('a', 'b'), ('c', 'd'), ('e', 'f'))))
True
>>> r2 == str(OrderedDict((('a', 'b'), ('e', 'f'), ('c', 'd'))))
True
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.__setattr__
documentation:
Implemented so that accesses to `sequence` raise a warning and are
diverted to the new `setkeys` method.

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.__getattr__
documentation:
Implemented so that access to `sequence` raises a warning.

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict()
>>> d.sequence
[]
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.__deepcopy__
documentation:
To allow deepcopy to work with OrderedDict.

```pycon
>>> from copy import deepcopy
>>> a = OrderedDict([(1, 1), (2, 2), (3, 3)])
>>> a['test'] = {}
>>> b = deepcopy(a)
>>> b == a
True
>>> b is a
False
>>> a['test'] is b['test']
False
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.items
documentation:
`items` returns a list of tuples representing all the
`(key, value)` pairs in the dictionary.

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.items()
[(1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)]
>>> d.clear()
>>> d.items()
[]
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.keys
documentation:
Return a list of keys in the `OrderedDict`.

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.keys()
[1, 3, 2]
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.values
documentation:
Return a list of all the values in the OrderedDict.

Optionally you can pass in a list of values, which will replace the
current list. The value list must be the same len as the OrderedDict.

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.values()
[3, 2, 1]
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.iteritems
documentation:
```pycon
>>> ii = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1))).iteritems()
>>> ii.next()
(1, 3)
>>> ii.next()
(3, 2)
>>> ii.next()
(2, 1)
>>> ii.next()
Traceback (most recent call last):
StopIteration
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.iterkeys
documentation:
```pycon
>>> ii = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1))).iterkeys()
>>> ii.next()
1
>>> ii.next()
3
>>> ii.next()
2
>>> ii.next()
Traceback (most recent call last):
StopIteration
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.itervalues
documentation:
```pycon
>>> iv = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1))).itervalues()
>>> iv.next()
3
>>> iv.next()
2
>>> iv.next()
1
>>> iv.next()
Traceback (most recent call last):
StopIteration
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.pop
documentation:
No dict.pop in Python 2.2, gotta reimplement it

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.pop(3)
2
>>> d
OrderedDict([(1, 3), (2, 1)])
>>> d.pop(4)
Traceback (most recent call last):
KeyError: 4
>>> d.pop(4, 0)
0
>>> d.pop(4, 0, 1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
TypeError: pop expected at most 2 arguments, got 3
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.popitem
documentation:
Delete and return an item specified by index, not a random one as in
dict. The index is -1 by default (the last item).

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.popitem()
(2, 1)
>>> d
OrderedDict([(1, 3), (3, 2)])
>>> d.popitem(0)
(1, 3)
>>> OrderedDict().popitem()
Traceback (most recent call last):
KeyError: 'popitem(): dictionary is empty'
>>> d.popitem(2)
Traceback (most recent call last):
IndexError: popitem(): index 2 not valid
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.setdefault
documentation:
```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.setdefault(1)
3
>>> d.setdefault(4) is None
True
>>> d
OrderedDict([(1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1), (4, None)])
>>> d.setdefault(5, 0)
0
>>> d
OrderedDict([(1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1), (4, None), (5, 0)])
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.update
documentation:
Update from another OrderedDict or sequence of (key, value) pairs

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 0), (0, 1)))
>>> d.update(OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1))))
>>> d
OrderedDict([(1, 3), (0, 1), (3, 2), (2, 1)])
>>> d.update({4: 4})
Traceback (most recent call last):
TypeError: undefined order, cannot get items from dict
>>> d.update((4, 4))
Traceback (most recent call last):
TypeError: cannot convert dictionary update sequence element "4" to a 2-item sequence
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.rename
documentation:
Rename the key for a given value, without modifying sequence order.

For the case where new_key already exists this raise an exception,
since if new_key exists, it is ambiguous as to what happens to the
associated values, and the position of new_key in the sequence.

```pycon
>>> od = OrderedDict()
>>> od['a'] = 1
>>> od['b'] = 2
>>> od.items()
[('a', 1), ('b', 2)]
>>> od.rename('b', 'c')
>>> od.items()
[('a', 1), ('c', 2)]
>>> od.rename('c', 'a')
Traceback (most recent call last):
ValueError: New key already exists: 'a'
>>> od.rename('d', 'b')
Traceback (most recent call last):
KeyError: 'd'
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.setitems
documentation:
This method allows you to set the items in the dict.

It takes a list of tuples - of the same sort returned by the `items`
method.

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict()
>>> d.setitems(((3, 1), (2, 3), (1, 2)))
>>> d
OrderedDict([(3, 1), (2, 3), (1, 2)])
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.setkeys
documentation:
`setkeys` all ows you to pass in a new list of keys which will
replace the current set. This must contain the same set of keys, but
need not be in the same order.

If you pass in new keys that don't match, a `KeyError` will be
raised.

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.keys()
[1, 3, 2]
>>> d.setkeys((1, 2, 3))
>>> d
OrderedDict([(1, 3), (2, 1), (3, 2)])
>>> d.setkeys(['a', 'b', 'c'])
Traceback (most recent call last):
KeyError: 'Keylist is not the same as current keylist.'
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.setvalues
documentation:
You can pass in a list of values, which will replace the
current list. The value list must be the same len as the OrderedDict.

(Or a `ValueError` is raised.)

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.setvalues((1, 2, 3))
>>> d
OrderedDict([(1, 1), (3, 2), (2, 3)])
>>> d.setvalues([6])
Traceback (most recent call last):
ValueError: Value list is not the same length as the OrderedDict.
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.index
documentation:
Return the position of the specified key in the OrderedDict.

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.index(3)
1
>>> d.index(4)
Traceback (most recent call last):
ValueError: list.index(x): x not in list
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.insert
documentation:
Takes `index`, `key`, and `value` as arguments.

Sets `key` to `value`, so that `key` is at position `index` in
the OrderedDict.

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.insert(0, 4, 0)
>>> d
OrderedDict([(4, 0), (1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)])
>>> d.insert(0, 2, 1)
>>> d
OrderedDict([(2, 1), (4, 0), (1, 3), (3, 2)])
>>> d.insert(8, 8, 1)
>>> d
OrderedDict([(2, 1), (4, 0), (1, 3), (3, 2), (8, 1)])
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.reverse
documentation:
Reverse the order of the OrderedDict.

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.reverse()
>>> d
OrderedDict([(2, 1), (3, 2), (1, 3)])
```

methods.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.OrderedDict.sort
documentation:
Sort the key order in the OrderedDict.

This method takes the same arguments as the `list.sort` method on
your version of Python.

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((4, 1), (2, 2), (3, 3), (1, 4)))
>>> d.sort()
>>> d
OrderedDict([(1, 4), (2, 2), (3, 3), (4, 1)])
```

A class of dictionary that keeps the insertion order of keys.

All appropriate methods return keys, items, or values in an ordered way.

All normal dictionary methods are available. Update and comparison is
restricted to other OrderedDict objects.

Various sequence methods are available, including the ability to explicitly
mutate the key ordering.

\_\_contains\_\_ tests:

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3),))
>>> 1 in d
1
>>> 4 in d
0
```

\_\_getitem\_\_ tests:

```pycon
>>> OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))[2]
1
>>> OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))[4]
Traceback (most recent call last):
KeyError: 4
```

\_\_len\_\_ tests:

```pycon
>>> len(OrderedDict())
0
>>> len(OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1))))
3
```

get tests:

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.get(1)
3
>>> d.get(4) is None
1
>>> d.get(4, 5)
5
>>> d
OrderedDict([(1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)])
```

has_key tests:

```pycon
>>> d = OrderedDict(((1, 3), (3, 2), (2, 1)))
>>> d.has_key(1)
1
>>> d.has_key(4)
0
```

classes.name: wwpdb.apps.workmanager.workflow_access.OrderedDict.SequenceOrderedDict
documentation:
Experimental version of OrderedDict that has a custom object for `keys`,
`values`, and `items`.

These are callable sequence objects that work as methods, or can be
manipulated directly as sequences.

Test for `keys`, `items` and `values`.

```pycon
>>> d = SequenceOrderedDict(((1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)))
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
>>> d.keys
[1, 2, 3]
>>> d.keys()
[1, 2, 3]
>>> d.setkeys((3, 2, 1))
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(3, 4), (2, 3), (1, 2)])
>>> d.setkeys((1, 2, 3))
>>> d.keys[0]
1
>>> d.keys[:]
[1, 2, 3]
>>> d.keys[-1]
3
>>> d.keys[-2]
2
>>> d.keys[0:2] = [2, 1]
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(2, 3), (1, 2), (3, 4)])
>>> d.keys.reverse()
>>> d.keys
[3, 1, 2]
>>> d.keys = [1, 2, 3]
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
>>> d.keys = [3, 1, 2]
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(3, 4), (1, 2), (2, 3)])
>>> a = SequenceOrderedDict()
>>> b = SequenceOrderedDict()
>>> a.keys == b.keys
1
>>> a['a'] = 3
>>> a.keys == b.keys
0
>>> b['a'] = 3
>>> a.keys == b.keys
1
>>> b['b'] = 3
>>> a.keys == b.keys
0
>>> a.keys > b.keys
0
>>> a.keys < b.keys
1
>>> 'a' in a.keys
1
>>> len(b.keys)
2
>>> 'c' in d.keys
0
>>> 1 in d.keys
1
>>> [v for v in d.keys]
[3, 1, 2]
>>> d.keys.sort()
>>> d.keys
[1, 2, 3]
>>> d = SequenceOrderedDict(((1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)), strict=True)
>>> d.keys[::-1] = [1, 2, 3]
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(3, 4), (2, 3), (1, 2)])
>>> d.keys[:2]
[3, 2]
>>> d.keys[:2] = [1, 3]
Traceback (most recent call last):
KeyError: 'Keylist is not the same as current keylist.'
```

```pycon
>>> d = SequenceOrderedDict(((1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)))
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
>>> d.values
[2, 3, 4]
>>> d.values()
[2, 3, 4]
>>> d.setvalues((4, 3, 2))
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(1, 4), (2, 3), (3, 2)])
>>> d.values[::-1]
[2, 3, 4]
>>> d.values[0]
4
>>> d.values[-2]
3
>>> del d.values[0]
Traceback (most recent call last):
TypeError: Can't delete items from values
>>> d.values[::2] = [2, 4]
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
>>> 7 in d.values
0
>>> len(d.values)
3
>>> [val for val in d.values]
[2, 3, 4]
>>> d.values[-1] = 2
>>> d.values.count(2)
2
>>> d.values.index(2)
0
>>> d.values[-1] = 7
>>> d.values
[2, 3, 7]
>>> d.values.reverse()
>>> d.values
[7, 3, 2]
>>> d.values.sort()
>>> d.values
[2, 3, 7]
>>> d.values.append('anything')
Traceback (most recent call last):
TypeError: Can't append items to values
>>> d.values = (1, 2, 3)
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(1, 1), (2, 2), (3, 3)])
```

```pycon
>>> d = SequenceOrderedDict(((1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)))
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
>>> d.items()
[(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)]
>>> d.setitems([(3, 4), (2 ,3), (1, 2)])
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(3, 4), (2, 3), (1, 2)])
>>> d.items[0]
(3, 4)
>>> d.items[:-1]
[(3, 4), (2, 3)]
>>> d.items[1] = (6, 3)
>>> d.items
[(3, 4), (6, 3), (1, 2)]
>>> d.items[1:2] = [(9, 9)]
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(3, 4), (9, 9), (1, 2)])
>>> del d.items[1:2]
>>> d
SequenceOrderedDict([(3, 4), (1, 2)])
>>> (3, 4) in d.items
1
>>> (4, 3) in d.items
0
>>> len(d.items)
2
>>> [v for v in d.items]
[(3, 4), (1, 2)]
>>> d.items.count((3, 4))
1
>>> d.items.index((1, 2))
1
>>> d.items.index((2, 1))
Traceback (most recent call last):
ValueError: list.index(x): x not in list
>>> d.items.reverse()
>>> d.items
[(1, 2), (3, 4)]
>>> d.items.reverse()
>>> d.items.sort()
>>> d.items
[(1, 2), (3, 4)]
>>> d.items.append((5, 6))
>>> d.items
[(1, 2), (3, 4), (5, 6)]
>>> d.items.insert(0, (0, 0))
>>> d.items
[(0, 0), (1, 2), (3, 4), (5, 6)]
>>> d.items.insert(-1, (7, 8))
>>> d.items
[(0, 0), (1, 2), (3, 4), (7, 8), (5, 6)]
>>> d.items.pop()
(5, 6)
>>> d.items
[(0, 0), (1, 2), (3, 4), (7, 8)]
>>> d.items.remove((1, 2))
>>> d.items
[(0, 0), (3, 4), (7, 8)]
>>> d.items.extend([(1, 2), (5, 6)])
>>> d.items
[(0, 0), (3, 4), (7, 8), (1, 2), (5, 6)]
```
